description: Synthetic HL7 v2.4 ORU^R01 CBC result from an EDAN H99S analyzer completing a WORKLIST-DRIVEN sample (LIS-149 AC3 return leg), modelled on the EDAN H90-series LIS Communication Protocol KB §3.2.3 (OBR-20 'Patient ID or Barcode') and the §6.1 worked example. Sibling of edan-h99s-oru-r01 (the direct-attach shape, OBR-2 IS the accession, no OBR-20): here OBR-2 carries the analyzer's OWN sample counter (13, meaningless off-instrument) and OBR-20 carries the barcode the host scanned and echoed back on the worklist ORF (see edan-h99s-worklist-query-qry-r02) — the join key the analyzer hands back unmodified. NOT yet captured on real H99S wire (the H99S ORUs captured so far are MSH-only connection-test pings); spec-backed + H60S-corroborated (the real H60S bench OBR-20 == OBR-2, i.e. carries the same identifier as the accession, consistent with OBR-20 being the scanned-barcode field there too). Replaced by a real non-blank bench capture at conformance (LIS-75).

source.reference: synthetic seed (LIS-149 AC3 — EDAN H90-series LIS Communication Protocol KB EDAN\WI\82-01.54.460907 v1.0, §3.2.3 OBR-20 repurposing / §6.1 ORU worked example). Device sub-type 507 per §7.
source.note: Application payload only (no MLLP framing). Models the worklist-driven shape: the analyzer accepted an order-download (see edan-h99s-worklist-query-qry-r02) whose ORF echoed barcode DEV01260000000000005 in OBR-20, and now reports the result against its OWN sample counter (OBR-2 = 13) while OBR-20 still carries that same barcode — the only reliable join key back to the OpenELIS order (the OBR-2 counter resets/collides across runs and is never an accession). CBC panel/values reused from edan-h99s-oru-r01 for parity. A real EDAN H99S bench capture of a worklist-driven result replaces this at conformance, per docs/runbooks/edan-h99s-bench-conformance.md.

current_parse.edge_sim: specimen=DEV01260000000000005 (OBR-20, preferred over the OBR-2 sample counter '13' for EDAN worklist-driven results); barcode=DEV01260000000000005 (same field, surfaced separately so a caller can tell a worklist-driven result from a direct-attach one, where barcode is blank); qc_type='' (OBR-20 is the barcode here, not an SD1 QC type/level); all 6 OBX read the code from OBX-4 -> WBC/RBC/HGB/HCT/MCV/PLT, each NORMALIZED to LOINC/UCUM.
current_parse.bridge: accession reconciled from barcode DEV01260000000000005 via the OE order-menu lookup (BarcodeAccessionResolver); controlLevel=null (the barcode must never leak into the SD1 QC-level field).

parser_gaps.detail: A worklist-driven EDAN H90-series result carries the analyzer's OWN sample counter in OBR-2 (here '13') and the scanned barcode (the join key echoed on the worklist ORF) in OBR-20. BEFORE this profile, oru.py._specimen_id(edan) read OBR-2 unconditionally, so a worklist-driven result would key on the meaningless counter and orphan. CLOSED by LIS-149 AC3: _specimen_id prefers a stripped-non-blank OBR-20 for EDAN, falling back to OBR-2 only when OBR-20 is absent/blank (the edan-h99s-oru-r01 direct-attach shape, which has no OBR-20). The sim has no OE order-menu lookup — unlike the production bridge (BarcodeAccessionResolver), the barcode IS the join key here.

OBR-20 is the Seamaty SD1's QC type/level field (oru.py qc_type). For EDAN, OBR-20 means something else entirely (the scanned barcode) and must never be read as a QC type/level. CLOSED: qc_type is forced to '' for every EDAN message, regardless of OBR-20 content; non-EDAN analyzers are unaffected.
